objective: Establish a correct baseline and perform one small local optimization probe on KernelBuilder.build to reduce instruction count without changing control flow or correctness.
invariants: Must preserve exact instruction sequence for correctness (debug/pause alignment)
Must not modify problem.py, tests/, or benchmark constants
Must keep N_CORES=1 and multicore disabled
known_facts: perf_takehome.py is the only writable file
HASH_STAGES is imported from problem.py and defines 6 hash stages
build() currently creates one instruction bundle per slot, which is inefficient for VLIW

task_graph.title: Establish baseline correctness and metric
task_graph.expected_signal: CYCLES: 147734 (or close), exit 0, all tests pass

last_observation.recovery_hint: Retry the same active task using only its declared contract.

task_graph.decision_hint: contract_drift_streak_deferred: active-task drift repeated after the contract rewrite budget was exhausted. Keep this task out of CODE and schedule a different runnable task.
task_graph.edit_scope: Run existing code to record baseline CYCLES and verify tests pass

risk_evidence.quote: Run existing code to record baseline CYCLES and verify tests pass
risk_evidence.explanation: This task involves no code changes, only execution and verification, so it is purely local with no structural risk.

validator.failure_condition: Exit code != 0 OR CYCLES line not found OR test failure

task_graph.correctness_rationale: No code changes are made; only measurement and verification.
task_graph.fallback_plan: Abandon if baseline cannot be established (e.g., syntax error in existing code).

task_graph.title: Optimize KernelBuilder.build to pack slots into VLIW bundles

last_observation.recovery_hint: Retry the same active task using only its declared contract.

task_graph.decision_hint: graph_rewrite_rejected: SPEC rewrite would collapse or delete the runnable portfolio. Keep sibling tasks and retry only with a materially narrower replacement.
preserved_invariants: Instruction count per engine must not exceed SLOT_LIMITS
Order of instructions must remain identical for correctness
task_graph.edit_scope: Modify build() to group slots into instruction bundles respecting SLOT_LIMITS instead of one bundle per slot.

risk_evidence.quote: Modify build() to group slots into instruction bundles respecting SLOT_LIMITS instead of one bundle per slot.
risk_evidence.explanation: Changing how instructions are bundled affects the control flow of the simulator (how many cycles each instruction takes), which is a structural change to execution.

task_graph.probe_plan: Implement a guarded version of build() that packs slots into bundles but keeps the original behavior if SLOT_LIMITS are exceeded or if correctness fails. Start with a simple packing strategy.

probe_diff_contract.observation: CYCLES count from do_kernel_test and test pass rate

invariant_evidence: SLOT_LIMITS are respected by the new packing logic
Instruction order is preserved within each engine
Debug/pause instructions remain in their original positions relative to other instructions

validator.failure_condition: CYCLES >= 147734 OR test failure OR SLOT_LIMITS violation detected

task_graph.correctness_rationale: By preserving instruction order and respecting slot limits, the semantic behavior of the kernel remains identical. The only change is execution parallelism within cycles.
task_graph.fallback_plan: If packing fails to reduce cycles or breaks correctness, revert to original build() implementation.
task_graph.rollback_or_shrink_plan: Shrink to a simpler packing strategy (e.g., pack only ALU instructions) or revert entirely if the probe fails.

issues: targeted SPEC rewrite left only one broad structural task

decision_rules: If task-002 fails to reduce CYCLES or breaks correctness, revert the change and try a smaller probe (e.g., only pack ALU instructions).
If baseline metric cannot be established in task-001, abort the spec.
Do not modify any files other than perf_takehome.py.
